text: Chemical Engineering prepares students to design and optimize sustainable processes for producing energy, materials, food, pharmaceuticals, and biomedical devices. The program emphasizes both large-scale manufacturing and micro-scale chemical processes.

Chemistry studies the composition, structure, and transformation of matter. The program provides strong foundations for careers in science and technology and offers access to advanced research facilities.

Civil Engineering focuses on designing and maintaining infrastructure while addressing environmental and climate challenges. Students gain expertise in environmental, geotechnical, hydrotechnical, and structural engineering.

Classical Studies examines ancient Greek and Roman civilizations through language, history, mythology, literature, philosophy, art, and archaeology, emphasizing multidisciplinary inquiry and cultural understanding.

Cognitive Science integrates psychology, philosophy, linguistics, neuroscience, and computing to study intelligence, cognition, and the mind. The program explores both human and artificial intelligence.

The Bachelor of Commerce program develops leadership, management, and teamwork skills. Students can personalize their degree through certificates, study abroad opportunities, and experiential learning.

The Commerce and Law combined program integrates business education with legal training, reducing completion time by one year. Students gain interdisciplinary expertise in business management and law.

Computer Engineering focuses on developing information and communication technologies connecting physical and digital systems. Students can specialize in areas such as artificial intelligence, software engineering, and computer hardware.
Computer Science provides broad training in computing principles with opportunities to specialize in areas such as artificial intelligence, programming languages, and human-computer interaction.
Computing explores software design, artificial intelligence, game development, and biomedical computation. The program offers flexible pathways and interdisciplinary opportunities.

Computing and the Creative Arts integrates computer science with fine arts disciplines such as art, drama, film, and music. Graduates pursue careers in entertainment, multimedia design, museums, and creative technology.

The Computing, Mathematics, and Analytics specialization is designed for students interested in graduate study or mathematically intensive computing fields such as optimization, security, communications, and biomedical computing. The program integrates computer science and mathematics to prepare students for advanced research and industry careers.

The Electrical and Computer Engineering Innovation Stream combines core engineering education with entrepreneurship and business training. Students develop innovation skills through team-based learning and industry engagement starting in first year.

metadata.program_name: Electrical and Computer Engineering – Innovation Stream

text: Electrical Engineering spans applied mathematics, physics, and information systems. Students may specialize in areas such as robotics, biomedical engineering, power systems, photonics, and artificial intelligence.
Employment Relations focuses on workplace relationships, labour law, and employment policy. The program examines equity, inclusion, and the human dimensions of work in a changing economy.

Engineering Chemistry uniquely integrates chemistry and engineering, preparing graduates as both professional engineers and chartered chemists. The program emphasizes diagnostics, alternative energy, and chemical process design.

Engineering Physics combines advanced physics with engineering problem-solving. Students gain expertise in quantum mechanics, nanotechnology, optics, and instrumentation for high-impact engineering careers.

The Professional Certificate in Enterprise Sales prepares learners for careers in sales, consulting, and business development. The program covers enterprise selling, account management, and sales analytics through applied learning.

The Entrepreneurship, Innovation and Creativity certificate fosters entrepreneurial thinking across disciplines. Students develop skills in innovation, finance, design thinking, and business planning.

Mathematical Physics provides rigorous training in theory, computation, and advanced mathematical modelling. Graduates develop highly transferable analytical skills applicable to careers in research, finance, medicine, technology, and data analytics.

The Mathematics program focuses on designing and analyzing mathematical models to better understand physical, biological, economic, and social phenomena. Students develop strong analytical and problem-solving skills applicable across many disciplines.

Mathematics and Engineering blends advanced mathematics with engineering concepts, preparing students to solve complex engineering problems in areas such as communications, artificial intelligence, control systems, and data science.

Mechanical and Materials Engineering covers the design, manufacturing, testing, and operation of mechanical systems. Undergraduate students may specialize in biomechanical or materials engineering, while graduate programs support research and professional development.

Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering focuses on intelligent autonomous systems, robotics, control systems, and artificial intelligence. Students work on hands-on projects and can specialize in areas such as automation, biomedical engineering, or intelligent systems.

Media and Performance Production is an interdisciplinary program combining film, drama, and music production. Students explore media creation, sound, performance, and digital arts with a focus on cross-disciplinary collaboration.

metadata.faculty: Arts and Science; DAN School of Drama & Music

text: Mining Engineering explores mineral extraction, processing, and environmental management. Undergraduate, graduate, and professional development options prepare students for careers in a global and technologically evolving mining industry.

The Certificate in Mining Technology is a fully online, asynchronous program designed for professionals and students entering the mining sector. It develops modern mining competencies through six undergraduate-level courses.
